description:
# Automatic Issue Refresh

## Goal

Make Beadsmith's Issue List stay in sync with Beadwork automatically — no user action required, no manual refresh button. When an issue is created, closed, deferred, or otherwise changed in the current workspace by any actor that updates the local `refs/heads/beadwork` ref for that workspace (the user in another terminal, a coding agent, a `bw sync`), the Beadsmith UI should reflect the new state within a few seconds.

## Context

Beadwork stores issue data on a git orphan branch named `beadwork`, scoped to the fully qualified ref `refs/heads/beadwork`. The data is not in the working tree; the canonical "data changed" signal is the SHA of that ref. Beadsmith currently loads the Issue List exactly once on mount, despite the glossary calling it a "live collection."

The full design is in ADR-0007.

## Approach (per ADR-0007)

A refresh service in the Rust backend, bound to the Current Workspace, with three trigger sources routing through one single-flight loader. The service is idle in the empty-workspace state (no Current Workspace per ADR-0006).

- **Data change:** every ~2 seconds, run `git -C <workspace> rev-parse --verify refs/heads/beadwork^{commit}` and compare to the last observed SHA for the current workspace.
- **Time tick:** every 60 seconds, a low-cadence timer fires so time-sensitive Ready changes (`defer_until` expiry) surface without a ref movement.
- **Window focus gain:** when the window gains focus, refresh once, skipping the first focus event of a session.
- **Single-flight loader:** all three triggers route through one loader that runs the existing Beadwork loaders (`bw list --all --json`, `bw ready --json`, `bw blocked --json`) and emits a Tauri event. If a reload is already in flight, a new trigger sets a dirty flag and the loader re-runs once after the active load completes if the data is still stale.
- **Workspace generation guard:** every emitted event carries a workspace path and a generation token. The frontend drops events whose generation does not match the active one, so an in-flight loader from a previous workspace cannot overwrite the new workspace's state.
- **Error handling:** transient errors (ref probe, loader) follow a 5-strike rule (silent until 5 consecutive failures); structural errors (`NotBeadworkWorkspace`, missing `git` or `bw`) surface immediately. Both render as a banner above the list, not a full replacement.
- **Workspace switch:** the switch triggers an immediate load for the new workspace, then the poller takes over. A per-workspace `last_seen_sha` map skips reloads on re-visits with no change.
- Silent re-render UX; no manual button.

## Data flow

```mermaid
graph LR
  Poller[2s git rev-parse] --> SHA{SHA changed?}
  SHA -->|Yes| Loader[Single-flight bw loaders]
  SHA -->|No| Wait[Wait 2s]
  Loader --> Event[Emit Tauri event with workspace path + generation token]
  Event --> FrontGuard{Generation matches current?}
  FrontGuard -->|No| Drop[Drop event]
  FrontGuard -->|Yes| State[Replace IssueExplorerLoadState]
  Wait --> Poller
  Ticker[60s timer] --> Loader
  Focus[WindowEvent::Focused gain] --> Loader
```

The data flow graph shows the happy path plus the workspace generation guard. Single-flight semantics, per-class error handling, and the 5-strike rule are documented in ADR-0007.

## Acceptance criteria

- Mutating a Beadwork issue from outside the app (e.g. `bw create` in another terminal) causes the Issue List to update in the running Beadsmith window within ~3 seconds.
- A deferred issue whose `defer_until` boundary passes while Beadsmith is open transitions into Ready within 60 seconds without a ref change.
- Returning to a backgrounded Beadsmith window (focus event) triggers an immediate refresh.
- Switching to a different workspace shows that workspace's current state; the switch triggers an immediate load (no need to wait for the next 2s tick).
- A persistent transient error (5+ consecutive failures) or a structural error (`NotBeadworkWorkspace`, missing `git` or `bw`) shows a banner above the list, not a full replacement. A brief transient error does not surface at all.
- After a workspace switch, any late-arriving refresh event from the previous workspace's poller is dropped; the new workspace's state is never overwritten by stale data.
- The user sees no spinner, toast, or other indicator on automatic refresh — the list updates in place.
- There is no manual refresh button in the UI.

## Failure UX for missing dependencies

If `git` is missing or not on PATH, Beadsmith shows a banner explaining it needs `git` to detect changes automatically. (Beadsmith has historically only required `bw`; this design extends that surface to `git` for the ref-tip probe.) Missing `bw` is handled with the same banner pattern.

## Test expectations

The implementation will need lower-level tests for the risky cases:

- **Fake clock** for 2s and 60s tick behavior (advance the clock, assert the loader runs at expected intervals).
- **Fake runner** for SHA changes (return a sequence of SHAs, assert reloads fire only on change).
- **In-flight + dirty race** (change SHA while a load is running, assert a single rerun after the active load completes if the ref still differs).
- **Workspace-switch generation guard** (an in-flight loader from workspace A completes after switch to B; assert the new state is not overwritten).
- **Transient vs structural failure** (fake runner returns transient errors, assert last good state is preserved; fake runner returns `NotBeadworkWorkspace`, assert banner renders immediately).
- **Frontend atomic replacement** (event payload N+1 arrives before N, assert the state is not regressed).

## Out of scope (flagged for later)

- Collapsing the three `bw` loaders into a single `bw state --json` call.
- Configurable polling interval.
- Pause-when-minimized behavior.
- A `bw watch` command in Beadwork (deferred to upstream).

## References

- ADR-0007 — Refresh issues by polling the Beadwork ref
- ADR-0002 — Beadwork is the source of truth
- ADR-0003 — Integrate through bw CLI output
- ADR-0005 — Use Beadwork CLI views for Ready and Blocked
- ADR-0006 — Workspace management and switching
- Glossary: Issue, Issue List, Ready, Workspace, Current Workspace
title: Automatically refresh issues from Beadwork
